message:
feat(core,parser,layout,render-term): add gantt chart metadata and expand sequence rendering

Gantt charts:
- Add IrGanttMeta structures for date-aware gantt chart metadata
- Parse task types, date formats, exclusions in mermaid_parser
- Update layout and terminal renderer for gantt elements

Sequence diagrams:
- Continue layout and rendering improvements

Tests:
- Update gantt_basic.svg golden fixture
- Expand integration_test.rs with new test cases
- Update beads issue tracking

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1,4 +1,4 @@
-{"id":"bd-0bu2","title":"Implement IrGanttMeta for gantt chart date-aware features","description":"# Gantt Chart IR Metadata

## Purpose
Gantt charts need date-aware metadata that the generic IR doesn't support. This task adds gantt-specific metadata structures.

## Types to Add
1. IrGanttMeta { date_format: String, title: Option<String>, excludes: Vec<GanttExclude>, sections: Vec<IrGanttSection> }
2. IrGanttSection { name: String, tasks: Vec<IrGanttTask> }
3. IrGanttTask { id: String, name: String, task_type: GanttTaskType, start: GanttDate, end: GanttDate, depends_on: Vec<String>, progress: Option<f32> }
4. GanttTaskType enum: Normal, Critical, Done, Active, Milestone
5. GanttDate enum: Absolute(String), AfterTask(String), Duration(String)
6. GanttExclude enum: Weekends, Dates(Vec<String>)
7. Add gantt_meta: Option<IrGanttMeta> to MermaidDiagramIr

 {"id":"bd-0n3y","title":"Sequence: support autonumber start and increment parity","description":"# Sequence autonumber parameter parity

Mermaid sequence diagrams support more than bare `autonumber`; they also support explicit numeric parameters such as `autonumber 10` and `autonumber 10 5`.

Scope
- Parse `autonumber <start>` and `autonumber <start> <increment>` in sequence diagrams
- Store the starting number and increment in sequence IR metadata
- Render numbered sequence message labels using the configured progression instead of always starting at 1 with step 1
- Preserve existing bare `autonumber` behavior as start=1 increment=1
- Add parser/core/render regression coverage

Acceptance Criteria
- No unsupported-syntax warning for parameterized `autonumber` lines
- IR round-trips the configured numbering parameters cleanly
- SVG numbering reflects configured start and increment deterministically
- Focused tests plus relevant workspace quality gates pass via `rch` for cargo operations","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:27:16.938161393Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-23T20:36:17.108872718Z","closed_at":"2026-03-23T20:36:17.108376919Z","close_reason":"Implemented sequence autonumber parameter parity","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-0pi0","title":"Packet-Beta: Implement bit numbering and field type rendering","description":"# Packet-Beta Rendering Improvements

## Gaps
- Bit numbering along top/bottom of packet header not rendered
- Field type/description text not rendered within fields
- Byte alignment grid not rendered
- Field styling by type

## Implementation Plan
1. Render bit numbering as small labels along the top edge of the packet
2. Show field name and optional type centered within each field box
3. Draw byte alignment markers at 8-bit boundaries
4. Apply distinct fill colors for different field types if categorized","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:47:11.697546308Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T05:42:24.100084908Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["packet-beta","parity","render"],"comments":[{"id":167,"issue_id":"bd-0pi0","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

4. Field spanning entire row → verify full-width rendering

 {"id":"bd-106","title":"EPIC: Complete Parser for All Mermaid Diagram Types","description":"Expand fm-parser to support ALL 24+ mermaid diagram types that mermaid-js supports. The existing FrankenTUI parser handles ~8 types (flowchart, sequence, class, state, gantt, pie, quadrant, packet). We need to add: ER diagram, git graph, mindmap, timeline, user journey, sankey, C4, kanban, architecture, block diagram, radar chart, XY chart, treemap, requirement diagram, plus the DOT format bridge. Each new diagram type needs: tokenizer rules, AST types in fm-core, parser producing MermaidDiagramIr, and test fixtures. Additionally, implement graceful error recovery so malformed/corrupted input degrades to best-effort rendering rather than failing. This is where we differentiate from mermaid-js by being SMARTER about user intent.

## Success Criteria

- Every capability listed in this epic is delivered completely through child issues; no scope cuts and no loss of planned functionality.
- Child tasks include comprehensive unit/integration coverage and e2e coverage with detailed logging; test evidence is available and reproducible.
- Performance, determinism, and reliability expectations for this epic are validated with measurable checks.
- User-facing behavior is documented (README/docs/config/migration notes) for all newly shipped functionality.
- Release readiness is proven by passing workspace quality gates and epic-level validation flows end-to-end.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-11T16:30:01.329811699Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T07:04:33.693357514Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T07:04:33.692624085Z","close_reason":"All 24 Mermaid diagram type parsers are implemented: flowchart, sequence, class, state, ER, gitgraph, gantt, pie, mindmap, timeline, journey, quadrant, sankey, C4 (5 variants), architecture, block-beta, packet, kanban, xychart, requirement. Detection with fuzzy matching and content heuristics also complete.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["epic","parser"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-106","depends_on_id":"bd-3bc.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-xwm3","title":"Implement subgraph direction override","description":"# Subgraph Direction Override

## What mermaid-js Supports
\`\`\`
flowchart LR
  subgraph sub1
    direction TB
    A --> B
  end
  sub1 --> C
\`\`\`
Each subgraph can have its own direction that overrides the parent graph direction. This affects layout within that subgraph only.

## Current State
- The \`direction\` keyword IS parsed within subgraphs
- The direction is stored on IrSubgraph
- The layout engine does NOT honor per-subgraph direction — it uses a single global direction

## Implementation Plan
1. **Layout** (fm-layout/src/lib.rs): In the Sugiyama layout, when processing nodes within a subgraph/cluster, check if the cluster has a direction override. If so, apply rank assignment and coordinate assignment with that direction for nodes within the cluster.
2. **Tests**: Create a flowchart with mixed directions (parent LR, child TB). Verify child nodes are laid out vertically within the horizontal parent flow.

## Complexity
This is a non-trivial layout change. The current Sugiyama implementation uses a single global direction. Supporting per-subgraph direction requires partitioning the layout into independent sub-layouts and stitching them together. Consider a phased approach: first support the feature in the IR, then implement the layout logic.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:43:18.742818041Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-24T04:16:31.599729614Z","closed_at":"2026-03-24T04:16:31.599220411Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["flowchart","layout","parity","parser"],"comments":[{"id":142,"issue_id":"bd-xwm3","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

### E2E Validation Script
Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason.

### Quality Gates

- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test --workspace (zero new failures)
","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:30:40Z"},{"id":201,"issue_id":"bd-xwm3","author":"ubuntu","text":"IMPLEMENTATION DETAIL: IrSubgraph in fm-core currently has NO direction field. Step 0 is to add 'direction: Option<GraphDirection>' to IrSubgraph. Then the parser can store per-subgraph direction, and the layout can read it.","created_at":"2026-03-17T06:27:08Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-y2qs","title":"Implement class member visibility modifiers and type annotations","description":"# Class Member Visibility and Type Annotations

## What mermaid-js Supports
\`\`\`
classDiagram
  class Animal {
    +String name
    -int age
    #List~Food~ diet
    ~void breathe()
    +eat(Food food) bool
    +sleep()* : void
  }
\`\`\`
- \`+\` public, \`-\` private, \`#\` protected, \`~\` package
- Type before name for attributes, after parentheses for return types
- \`*\` suffix for abstract methods
- \`$\` suffix for static methods

## Current State
- Class blocks ARE parsed, member lines are captured
- Visibility modifiers are NOT extracted — stored as raw text in member string
- Type annotations are NOT parsed separately from names
- No rendering of visibility symbols

## Implementation Plan
1. **Parser**: When parsing class block members, extract leading +/-/#/~ as visibility. Parse \`type name\` for attributes, \`name(params) returnType\` for methods. Detect */$ suffixes.
2. **IR**: Add IrClassMember { visibility: Visibility, name: String, return_type: Option<String>, parameters: Vec<String>, is_abstract: bool, is_static: bool }.
3. **SVG Renderer**: Render three-compartment class boxes: header (class name + stereotype), attributes section, methods section. Prepend visibility symbol. Show types.
4. **Tests**: All visibility markers, attribute types, method signatures, abstract/static.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:44:44.660117013Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T09:33:44.181153568Z","closed_at":"2026-03-21T09:33:44.180655345Z","close_reason":"Implemented class member visibility + three-compartment rendering. Parser already extracts +/-/#/~ visibility, types, static/abstract. Terminal renderer shows UML class boxes with compartment separators. SVG renderer shows three-compartment boxes with stereotypes, visibility symbols, type annotations, and separator lines.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["class","parity","parser","render"],"comments":[{"id":149,"issue_id":"bd-y2qs","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

- Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics

### E2E Validation Script
Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason.

### Quality Gates

 {"id":"bd-y6o8","title":"Implement style and linkStyle directive parsing and rendering","description":"# style / linkStyle Directive Support

## What mermaid-js Supports
Inline styling for specific nodes and edges:
\`\`\`
style nodeId fill:#f9f,stroke:#333,stroke-width:4px
linkStyle 0 stroke:#ff3,stroke-width:4px,color:red
linkStyle default stroke:#333
\`\`\`

style applies to a node by ID. linkStyle applies to an edge by index (0-based declaration order) or 'default' for all edges.

## Current State
- Parser recognizes style/linkStyle lines but does not capture the property map
- No rendering path for inline styles

## Implementation Plan
1. **Parser**: Parse \`style nodeId prop:value,...\` and store as IrNodeStyle on the IrNode. Parse \`linkStyle index prop:value,...\` and store as IrEdgeStyle on the IrEdge.
2. **IR**: Add \`inline_style: Option<IrInlineStyle>\` to IrNode and IrEdge. IrInlineStyle is a BTreeMap<String, String>.
3. **SVG Renderer**: Apply inline_style as SVG attributes (fill → rect fill, stroke → rect stroke, etc.). For edges, apply to <path> elements.
4. **Tests**: Verify style is applied in SVG output, verify linkStyle by index, verify default linkStyle.

## Considerations
- Property names must be mapped from CSS-like (stroke-width) to SVG attributes
- Sanitize values to prevent XSS in SVG output","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:43:18.486402415Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T05:42:23.800287285Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["flowchart","parity","parser","render"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-y6o8","depends_on_id":"bd-77gh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:48:23.246993348Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":140,"issue_id":"bd-y6o8","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:30:39Z"},{"id":185,"issue_id":"bd-y6o8","author":"ubuntu","text":"CROSS-DIAGRAM NOTE: style/linkStyle directives apply to flowchart primarily but the IR infrastructure should be generic enough for any diagram type. Also: style supports comma-separated node IDs: 'style nodeA,nodeB,nodeC fill:#f9f' applies to multiple nodes at once. The parser must split on commas and apply the style to each node.","created_at":"2026-03-17T05:42:23Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-yh4v","title":"Implement gantt date parsing, task types, and duration computation","description":"# Gantt Date/Duration/Task Type Parsing

## What mermaid-js Supports
\`\`\`
gantt
  dateFormat YYYY-MM-DD
  title Project Plan
  excludes weekends
  section Phase 1
  Design           :done, des1, 2024-01-01, 30d
  Implementation   :active, impl1, after des1, 60d
  Testing          :crit, test1, after impl1, 2024-05-01
  Milestone        :milestone, m1, after test1, 0d
\`\`\`
- dateFormat: YYYY-MM-DD, DD/MM/YYYY, etc.
- Task types: done, active, crit (critical), milestone
- Duration: Nd (days), after taskId (dependency), absolute dates
- excludes: weekends, specific dates

## Current State
- Parser recognizes sections and task lines, but does NOT extract dates, types, durations, or dependencies
- Layout has a gantt algorithm but operates on generic nodes, not time-aware bars

## Implementation Plan
1. **Parser**: After the colon in task lines, parse comma-separated metadata: type (done/active/crit/milestone), id, start (date or 'after id'), end (date or duration). Parse dateFormat directive. Parse excludes.
2. **IR**: Add IrGanttMeta { date_format, excludes, sections: Vec<IrGanttSection> } with IrGanttTask { id, name, task_type, start, end, depends_on }.
3. **Layout**: Compute actual x-positions from dates. Apply excludes to skip weekends. Position milestone as diamonds.
4. **Tests**: Date formats, durations, after-dependencies, excludes, milestones, crit tasks.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:46:07.294100500Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T06:26:31.708821785Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["gantt","parity","parser"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-yh4v","depends_on_id":"bd-0bu2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:48:28.205838996Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":155,"issue_id":"bd-yh4v","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

### Unit Tests

- Render output assertions (SVG element presence, attributes, structure)
- Edge cases: empty input, malformed input, boundary values

### Integration Tests
- Full pipeline: parse -> layout -> render for representative inputs
- Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs)
- Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics

### E2E Validation Script
Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason.

### Quality Gates
All must pass before this bead can be closed:
- cargo check --workspace --all-targets
- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings  
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test --workspace (zero new failures)
","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:30:41Z"},{"id":196,"issue_id":"bd-yh4v","author":"ubuntu","text":"ADDITIONAL GANTT DIRECTIVES TO PARSE:
- todayMarker stroke-width:5px,stroke:#0f0,opacity:0.5 → custom styling for the today line. Store as optional style string in IrGanttMeta.
- tickInterval 1day / 1week / 1month → controls axis label granularity. Store as enum (Day/Week/Month/Quarter/Year) in IrGanttMeta.
- inclusiveEndDates → when true, task end dates include the end day. Store as bool in IrGanttMeta.
- weekday monday → sets start of week. Store as optional u8 in IrGanttMeta.
- axisFormat %Y-%m-%d → date format for axis labels. Store as String in IrGanttMeta.

These must ALL be parsed for full parity. Update the IrGanttMeta type definition in bd-0bu2 accordingly.","created_at":"2026-03-17T06:26:31Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-yh4v","title":"Implement gantt date parsing, task types, and duration computation","description":"# Gantt Date/Duration/Task Type Parsing

## What mermaid-js Supports
\`\`\`
gantt
  dateFormat YYYY-MM-DD
  title Project Plan
  excludes weekends
  section Phase 1
  Design           :done, des1, 2024-01-01, 30d
  Implementation   :active, impl1, after des1, 60d
  Testing          :crit, test1, after impl1, 2024-05-01
  Milestone        :milestone, m1, after test1, 0d
\`\`\`
- dateFormat: YYYY-MM-DD, DD/MM/YYYY, etc.
- Task types: done, active, crit (critical), milestone
- Duration: Nd (days), after taskId (dependency), absolute dates
- excludes: weekends, specific dates

## Current State
- Parser recognizes sections and task lines, but does NOT extract dates, types, durations, or dependencies
- Layout has a gantt algorithm but operates on generic nodes, not time-aware bars

## Implementation Plan
1. **Parser**: After the colon in task lines, parse comma-separated metadata: type (done/active/crit/milestone), id, start (date or 'after id'), end (date or duration). Parse dateFormat directive. Parse excludes.
2. **IR**: Add IrGanttMeta { date_format, excludes, sections: Vec<IrGanttSection> } with IrGanttTask { id, name, task_type, start, end, depends_on }.
3. **Layout**: Compute actual x-positions from dates. Apply excludes to skip weekends. Position milestone as diamonds.
4. **Tests**: Date formats, durations, after-dependencies, excludes, milestones, crit tasks.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:46:07.294100500Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-24T05:56:03.963956918Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["gantt","parity","parser"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-yh4v","depends_on_id":"bd-0bu2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:48:28.205838996Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":155,"issue_id":"bd-yh4v","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

This bead MUST ship with:

### Unit Tests
- Focused tests for every new parser path (positive + negative cases)
- Render output assertions (SVG element presence, attributes, structure)
- Edge cases: empty input, malformed input, boundary values

### Integration Tests
- Full pipeline: parse -> layout -> render for representative inputs
- Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs)
- Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics

### E2E Validation Script
Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason.

### Quality Gates
All must pass before this bead can be closed:
- cargo check --workspace --all-targets
- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings  
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test --workspace (zero new failures)
","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:30:41Z"},{"id":196,"issue_id":"bd-yh4v","author":"ubuntu","text":"ADDITIONAL GANTT DIRECTIVES TO PARSE:
- todayMarker stroke-width:5px,stroke:#0f0,opacity:0.5 → custom styling for the today line. Store as optional style string in IrGanttMeta.
- tickInterval 1day / 1week / 1month → controls axis label granularity. Store as enum (Day/Week/Month/Quarter/Year) in IrGanttMeta.
- inclusiveEndDates → when true, task end dates include the end day. Store as bool in IrGanttMeta.
- weekday monday → sets start of week. Store as optional u8 in IrGanttMeta.
- axisFormat %Y-%m-%d → date format for axis labels. Store as String in IrGanttMeta.

These must ALL be parsed for full parity. Update the IrGanttMeta type definition in bd-0bu2 accordingly.","created_at":"2026-03-17T06:26:31Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-zi0e","title":"Sequence: support named box colors and transparent box syntax parity","description":"# Sequence box color parity

## Goal
Support Mermaid sequence box declarations that use named colors and the special transparent form, matching current docs examples like `box Aqua Group Description` and `box transparent Aqua`.

## Scope
- Parse leading named CSS color tokens in sequence `box` declarations, not just hex/rgb/hsl forms.
- Treat `box transparent <label>` as transparent box fill with `<label>` preserved as the label.
- Preserve the parsed group color through sequence IR/layout to SVG rendering for participant group clusters.
- Add focused parser/layout/render tests.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `box Aqua Group` stores color `Aqua` and label `Group`.
- `box transparent Aqua` stores a transparent color and label `Aqua`.
- SVG uses the parsed group color without unsafe attribute injection.
- Quality gates for touched crates pass via `rch` and formatting is clean.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:41:43.328437882Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-23T20:48:15.194234898Z","closed_at":"2026-03-23T20:48:15.193746754Z","close_reason":"Implemented sequence box color parity","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-zmbp","title":"State: Complete feature parity with mermaid-js","description":"# State Diagram Feature Parity

## Current State
Basic states and transitions work (~75% parser). Missing all UML pseudostate types that make state diagrams expressive.

## Gaps
1. Start/end pseudo-state special rendering ([*])
2. Fork/join parallel bars
3. Choice pseudo-state (diamond shape)
4. History states (H / H*)
5. Composite/nested states with proper containment
6. Notes on states
7. Entry/exit actions on transitions
8. Guard conditions [guard] on transitions
9. State descriptions (state name : description)
10. Concurrency regions (-- separator within composite state)

## Reference
- mermaid-js docs: https://mermaid.js.org/syntax/stateDiagram.html","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:45:19.858400163Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:49.981662394Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["parity","state"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-zmbp","depends_on_id":"bd-2gwq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:48.302936595Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-zmbp","depends_on_id":"bd-qj2b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:49.980550820Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-zmbp","depends_on_id":"bd-zn9n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:49.128081199Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
